title: How I Wet Your Mother

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Opening of Simpsons episode
jokes.punchline: Homer's iconic frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Homer's signature catchphrase - perfect character integration and economy, iconic but not particularly original in this context

jokes.text: Item being procured: one standard-sized paper clip.
jokes.setup: Formal announcement of office supply acquisition
jokes.punchline: Bureaucratic language for stealing a single paperclip
jokes.explanation: Clever juxtaposition of formal language with petty theft - good structure and economy

jokes.text: See it? We followed the ref home and beat the crap out of him. Yeah, I still think that might've been a kid who worked at Foot Locker.
jokes.setup: Discussion of bad referee call escalates to vigilante justice
jokes.punchline: They beat up the wrong person - a Foot Locker employee
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from sports complaint to assault, with perfect dark comedy twist that they attacked an innocent retail worker

jokes.text: Unguarded stuff...!
jokes.setup: Homer sees the open supply room
jokes.punchline: His primal excitement over free office supplies
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer moment - his childlike glee at petty theft opportunities

jokes.text: Attention, lovers of free office supplies! Come and steal things you can easily afford!
jokes.setup: Public announcement about office supplies
jokes.punchline: Openly encouraging theft of cheap items
jokes.explanation: Good ironic contrast between 'free' and 'steal' plus the absurd logic of stealing affordable items

jokes.text: Can someone open this bottle of mother wolf placenta for me?
jokes.setup: Mr. Burns needs help with something
jokes.punchline: It's a bizarre health product - wolf placenta
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns character moment - casually requesting help with something completely absurd and disgusting

jokes.text: My rubberized bands! My binder clips! My accordion Post-its!
jokes.setup: Homer discovers office supplies being stolen
jokes.punchline: His passionate attachment to mundane office items
jokes.explanation: Good escalation in specificity of office supplies, shows Homer's childlike attachment to these items

jokes.text: Oh, Lenny, why would you steal my bear? I just wanted something to cuddle at night. That's my cuddle bear! I loved him, I shot him-- he's mine!
jokes.setup: Homer confronts Lenny about stealing his bear
jokes.punchline: Homer's possessive relationship with a bear he killed
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy of Homer's emotional attachment to something he killed, juxtaposed with Lenny's innocent desire to cuddle

jokes.text: You don't even work here! Wha...?! You mean I've been calling in sick for nothing?
jokes.setup: Mr. Burns confronts office supply thieves
jokes.punchline: Someone's been calling in sick to a job they don't have
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - calling in sick to a non-existent job shows complete commitment to the bit

jokes.text: Wow, he threw us under the bus, and now he's sitting in the bus driver's lap.
jokes.setup: Homer gets rewarded while others get punished
jokes.punchline: Extended metaphor of Homer literally sitting in bus driver's lap
jokes.explanation: Clever extension of 'thrown under the bus' metaphor with vivid physical imagery

jokes.text: Well, sir, before I do anything, I stop and ask myself: 'What would Jesus and Mr. Burns do?'
jokes.setup: Homer explains his moral compass
jokes.punchline: Combining Jesus with the evil Mr. Burns as moral guides
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - combining the ultimate good and evil figures as dual moral authorities shows his confused worldview

jokes.text: You are garbage made flesh! This is a mockery!
jokes.setup: Homer's absurd moral statement
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top outraged reaction to Homer's hypocrisy
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic overreaction, 'garbage made flesh' is a colorful insult

jokes.text: Question: Can we have fun with it? No, you may not.
jokes.setup: Workers assigned to write essays about Homer
jokes.punchline: Burns' flat denial of any creative freedom
jokes.explanation: Burns' authoritarian nature shown through denying even the possibility of fun in punishment

jokes.text: Hey, it's called karma. Isn't karma where if you do something bad, bad things happen to you? A common misconception.
jokes.setup: Homer explains karma to Bart
jokes.punchline: Homer dismisses the actual definition of karma as misconception
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - confidently dismissing the correct definition of karma because it doesn't fit his narrative

jokes.text: Suck it, karma! Yeah! I'm talking to you, karma! Ha-ha-ha! Karma's a bitch, karma!
jokes.setup: Homer taunts the concept of karma
jokes.punchline: Increasingly aggressive taunting of an abstract concept
jokes.explanation: Homer's childish taunting of karma sets up the inevitable comeuppance

jokes.text: Ah, nothing better than a lazy Saturday morning lying in a warm, moist bed, because weekends are... Moist?! I wet the bed!
jokes.setup: Homer's pleasant morning commentary
jokes.punchline: Realization that 'moist' means he wet himself
jokes.explanation: Good setup with the word 'moist' leading to embarrassing realization - karma delivers immediately

jokes.text: Marge, from time to time I've heard you speak of a 'washing machine.' Where would I find this marvelous contraption?
jokes.setup: Homer needs to do laundry
jokes.punchline: Speaks of washing machine like it's exotic technology
jokes.explanation: Homer's complete unfamiliarity with basic household appliances despite being married with children

jokes.text: Not just the laundry. That would be weird, and you might ask questions.
jokes.setup: Homer offers to do chores
jokes.punchline: Admits doing normal husband duties would be suspicious
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-aware moment where Homer admits his normal behavior would be so out of character it would raise suspicion

jokes.text: Don't yell at me, brain. This happened on your watch. You have two jobs-- thinking and bladder control!
jokes.setup: Homer argues with his brain
jokes.punchline: Reduces brain function to just two basic tasks
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer internal monologue, reducing complex brain functions to two simple jobs like a performance review

jokes.text: I'm doing the best with what I got. All you feed me is reality shows! I like to watch Lamar Odom play Xbox while his giant wife yells at him. Is that so nuts?!
jokes.setup: Homer's brain defends itself
jokes.punchline: Brain's diet consists entirely of reality TV featuring Lamar Odom and Khloe Kardashian
jokes.explanation: Specific celebrity reference that was timely in 2012, brain defending its entertainment choices

jokes.text: Oh, why can't I cork my wang-wine?
jokes.setup: Homer's frustration with bedwetting
jokes.punchline: Creative euphemism combining 'wang' and 'wine' for urine
jokes.explanation: Crude but creative wordplay, though relies somewhat on shock value

jokes.text: But isn't karma just an expression of the dharma?
jokes.setup: Family discusses karma
jokes.punchline: Lisa's intellectual approach contrasts with family's ignorance
jokes.explanation: Typical Lisa moment showing her intellectual pretensions vs family's practical concerns

jokes.text: Who the hell are you? I'm sorry. Cheese on that? Sorry. Hey, Lenny. Sorry.
jokes.setup: Homer's apology party
jokes.punchline: Homer apologizing to random strangers while hosting
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's scattershot approach to apologizing, including to people he doesn't know

jokes.text: Make sure you whack 'piñata me' and not 'real me.'
jokes.setup: Homer has made a piñata of himself
jokes.punchline: Need to specify which Homer to hit
jokes.explanation: Funny visual concept of Homer making a piñata of himself and needing to clarify which one to attack

jokes.text: A lot of work went into this thing. Die! Die! Die!
jokes.setup: Homer proud of his piñata craftsmanship
jokes.punchline: Immediately encouraging its violent destruction
jokes.explanation: Ironic contrast between artistic pride and immediate destruction

jokes.text: Ain't no problem that free food and free booze won't fix.
jokes.setup: Homer asking for forgiveness
jokes.punchline: Carl's pragmatic philosophy about conflict resolution
jokes.explanation: Straightforward working-class wisdom about forgiveness being cheap when free stuff is involved

jokes.text: Free?! Uh... Oh... Free it is!
jokes.setup: Homer realizes he promised free food and drinks
jokes.punchline: Reluctant acceptance of unexpected expense
jokes.explanation: Homer's classic bumbling into promises he didn't intend to make

jokes.text: Tomorrow morning my sheets will be as dry as the surface of Mars, except for the poles.
jokes.setup: Homer confident about solving bedwetting
jokes.punchline: Unnecessarily specific and scientific comparison to Mars
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly scientific and specific metaphor from Homer, showing off random knowledge

jokes.text: Homer Simpson! You forgot your receipt for your adult bedwetting product! Homer Simpson! Are you there?!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to discreetly buy bedwetting products
jokes.punchline: Clerk loudly announcing embarrassing purchase to entire area
jokes.explanation: Classic embarrassing public announcement trope, works for cringe comedy

jokes.text: Now, that's what I call looking out for number one.
jokes.setup: Homer sets up bedwetting alarm
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'number one' meaning both self-interest and urination
jokes.explanation: Decent pun playing on multiple meanings of 'number one' - economical delivery

jokes.text: Uh, uh, that was just the fire alarm. Try to go back to sleep.
jokes.setup: Bedwetting alarm goes off
jokes.punchline: Homer's obviously false excuse about fire alarm
jokes.explanation: Homer's predictable bad lying, though fits his character

jokes.text: Son, I'm afraid the Uralarm Whiz-no-more 9000 is no joke.
jokes.setup: Bart questions if this is a joke
jokes.punchline: Serious delivery of ridiculous product name
jokes.explanation: Excellent fake product name combining medical terminology with obvious puns

jokes.text: Your mother and I are wetting the bed. 'We're' wetting the bed? Hey, when you were pregnant, everything was 'we.'
jokes.setup: Homer shares family secret awkwardly
jokes.punchline: Homer's logic about shared responsibility during pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Good callback to pregnancy 'we' language showing Homer's selective application of shared responsibility

jokes.text: I'm so turned on.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Homer's bedwetting problem
jokes.punchline: Marge's obvious sarcasm about finding it attractive
jokes.explanation: Simple sarcastic response, economical but not particularly creative

jokes.text: A diaper just isn't sexy. What about Cupid? He's smokin' hot. He's a baby with wings.
jokes.setup: Marge says diapers aren't sexy
jokes.punchline: Homer's bizarre counterargument using Cupid
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd logic from Homer - finding a way to make diapers sexy through mythological figures

jokes.text: Marge, it's not the diaper, it's what's inside.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to be romantic about diapers
jokes.punchline: Completely missing why this makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Homer's attempt at romantic sentiment makes the situation exponentially worse

jokes.text: I was just trying to get past the New York Times pay-wall, and then kaboy!
jokes.setup: Frink explains why he's outside at night
jokes.punchline: Frustrated by newspaper paywall leading to lab explosion
jokes.explanation: Topical 2012 reference to newspaper paywalls, good character moment for Frink

jokes.text: Tweeted by Bart, re-tweeted by Krusty.
jokes.setup: How Frink knows about Homer's problem
jokes.punchline: Family secrets spread through social media chain
jokes.explanation: Modern social media twist on gossip spreading, economically delivered

jokes.text: Normal Stu likes normal things! Hmm.
jokes.setup: Frink demonstrates his dream device success
jokes.punchline: Character now obsessed with being aggressively normal
jokes.explanation: Funny character name and concept - someone cured of unusual obsessions now obsessed with normalcy

jokes.text: What am I, a babysitter?! So no school for me.
jokes.setup: Maggie has to come along on dream mission
jokes.punchline: Frink's indignation vs Bart's excitement about missing school
jokes.explanation: Contrasting reactions show different priorities - Frink's professional annoyance vs Bart's opportunism

jokes.text: My brain's fine. In my dreams, I'm an intermediate skier!
jokes.setup: Family wants to fix Homer's brain
jokes.punchline: Homer's low bar for mental achievement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - extremely modest dream achievement shows his limited ambitions

jokes.text: It's Death! I recognize him from 40th birthday cards.
jokes.setup: Death appears in Homer's dream
jokes.punchline: Homer knows Death from over-the-hill birthday cards
jokes.explanation: Clever reference to morbid humor on milestone birthday cards, very Homer observation

jokes.text: You wet the bed?! Oh... great! Now Death knows!
jokes.setup: Death discovers Homer's secret shame
jokes.punchline: Homer's mortification that even Death knows his embarrassing secret
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - being judged by Death himself adds cosmic embarrassment to personal shame

jokes.text: Oh, uh, actually, because I neglected to install the latest Adobe Acrobat update, if you die in the dream, you die in real life.
jokes.setup: Everyone assumes dream death is safe
jokes.punchline: Death is real due to software update negligence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant modern twist on dream logic - life-or-death consequences due to mundane software maintenance failure

jokes.text: Incidentally, I've also proven that Hell is real, and everyone goes there. Frink out.
jokes.setup: Frink delivers bad news about dream death
jokes.punchline: Casually drops bombshell about Hell and signs off
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and dark comedy - casually revealing universal damnation as an afterthought

jokes.text: One minute at this level equals two hours one dream level below.
jokes.setup: Lisa explains dream mechanics
jokes.punchline: Reference to Inception movie's time dilation rules
jokes.explanation: Direct reference to Inception - clever for Lisa but relies heavily on pop culture knowledge

jokes.text: Wait, dreams have rules? Everything has rules, Bart. Not me, when I hit the dance floor.
jokes.setup: Discussion of dream rules
jokes.punchline: Homer's delusion about his dancing abilities
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's misplaced confidence in his dancing

jokes.text: Can the chatter and fetch me a baloney sandwich. Why don't you fetch it yourself, man.
jokes.setup: Abe makes demands in Homer's childhood dream
jokes.punchline: Bart's modern attitude clashing with 1950s dream
jokes.explanation: Good clash between different era parenting styles and modern kid attitude

jokes.text: See what your monkeyshines have done, boy? Still smells better than your gym socks, man.
jokes.setup: Abe blames Bart for ruining fish
jokes.punchline: Bart's dismissive insult about Abe's hygiene
jokes.explanation: Standard grandfather-grandson banter with generational conflict

jokes.text: Forsooth, a myst'ry doth confound... In your dreams.
jokes.setup: Lisa speaks in Shakespearean style
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal interpretation dismisses her as dreaming
jokes.explanation: Homer misses Lisa's attempt at literary language, treating 'in your dreams' literally

jokes.text: We never do my thing.
jokes.setup: Lisa's elaborate Shakespeare setup gets dismissed
jokes.punchline: Lisa's frustrated realization about being ignored
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment - intellectual middle child frustrated by family's lack of appreciation

jokes.text: In this fantasy Kwik-E-Mart, you get your change in bacon.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his fantasy world
jokes.punchline: Currency is bacon instead of money
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer fantasy - practical consumer experience improved by replacing money with bacon

jokes.text: In this place mothers are for drunk driving. Chug! Chug! Chug!
jokes.setup: Homer's fantasy world descriptions
jokes.punchline: Responsible Marge encouraged to drink and drive
jokes.explanation: Dark humor inverting Marge's responsible nature, though relies on shock value

jokes.text: Hi-diddly-ho, dream team! Where's my God now?
jokes.setup: Flanders appears in Homer's fantasy
jokes.punchline: Religious Flanders questioning God's existence in Homer's hedonistic dream
jokes.explanation: Excellent character subversion - Flanders' faith crisis in Homer's godless fantasy world

jokes.text: I take you to the Disneyland of me, and you just want to go to the lost and found.
jokes.setup: Marge wants to solve problems instead of enjoying fantasy
jokes.punchline: Homer's analogy comparing his psyche to an amusement park
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor - Homer's mind as Disneyland with Marge wanting to handle practical matters at lost and found

jokes.text: Unplug these people, Dr. Ker-dork-ian!
jokes.setup: Moe wants to wake everyone up from dream machine
jokes.punchline: Insult combining Dr. Kevorkian with 'dork'
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay on Dr. Kevorkian's name, fits Moe's crude insult style

jokes.text: Oh, you foolish man, if I unhook them now, I won't know if this is safe to use on chimps!
jokes.setup: Moe wants to stop the experiment
jokes.punchline: Frink's concern is about animal testing, not human safety
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy about scientific priorities - caring more about animal testing than human subjects

jokes.text: Quick! Gum up the gears with Moes.
jokes.setup: Need to stop dangerous machine
jokes.punchline: Using multiple Moes as mechanical impediment
jokes.explanation: Absurd visual of using people as machine parts

jokes.text: Hey, you know what's good for cleaning Moe gunk out of your gears? White vinegar. Yeah.
jokes.setup: Moe gets stuck in gears of dream machine
jokes.punchline: Moe giving practical cleaning advice about removing himself
jokes.explanation: Funny absurdist moment - Moe treating his own physical matter as a cleaning problem

jokes.text: May I ask, what's taken so long with Larry King? I am not... Death.
jokes.setup: Homer assumes he's talking to Death
jokes.punchline: It's actually his mother, plus Larry King death joke
jokes.explanation: Topical reference to Larry King's longevity, decent misdirection reveal

jokes.text: It's Jennifer Aniston's hair on Friends. Exactly like Chandler, always criticizing.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Mona's hair in dreams
jokes.punchline: Reference to Friends hairstyles and Chandler's sarcasm
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference humor, somewhat dated even for 2012

jokes.text: No need. With two Simpson men in the boat will bring you back a pile of fish. I'm in charge of the tackle box.
jokes.setup: Father-son fishing trip confidence
jokes.punchline: Young Homer's modest responsibility plus foreshadowing failure
jokes.explanation: Sets up the failure with overconfidence, young Homer's small role is endearing

jokes.text: Man Homer, you've always been a loser.
jokes.setup: Father-son fishing trip memory
jokes.punchline: Dad's brutal honesty to young Homer
jokes.explanation: Dark family humor - Abe's characteristic bluntness toward his son

jokes.text: It was just a TV show. Mitch Miller was yelling at one of his idiot singers.
jokes.setup: Young Homer heard parents fighting
jokes.punchline: Abe's specific cultural reference as excuse
jokes.explanation: Period-appropriate cultural reference for Abe's generation, realistic parental deflection

jokes.text: After we eat it, can we let it go?
jokes.setup: Discussion of catching fish
jokes.punchline: Child logic about eating then releasing fish
jokes.explanation: Perfect child logic showing young Homer's confused understanding of fishing consequences

jokes.text: Pathetic. A kid who can't keep his parents' marriage together is no kid at all.
jokes.setup: Homer realizes his guilt over parents' divorce
jokes.punchline: Absurd standard of childhood responsibility
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about impossible childhood expectations - blaming a kid for adult relationship failures

jokes.text: 'Roll the film, Cletus.' 'Kiss me, Cletus.' Whatever you say, boss lady.
jokes.setup: Mona asks Cletus to operate dream projector
jokes.punchline: Cletus's confused compliance with whatever he's told
jokes.explanation: Good Cletus character moment showing his simple willingness to follow any instruction

jokes.text: And maybe you'll stop overeating, too. No can do, baby.
jokes.setup: Mona suggests Homer might fix other problems
jokes.punchline: Homer draws the line at giving up food
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer priorities - willing to change many things but never food habits

jokes.text: Stanley Kubrick wanted Robin Williams to star in The Shining. Casablanca was originally going to star Ronald Reagan. There was a Grease 2, and I wasn't in it.
jokes.setup: Homer's brain contains movie trivia next to family memories
jokes.punchline: Escalation from real trivia to personal disappointment about Grease 2
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from real movie trivia to Homer's personal grievance about not being in Grease 2

jokes.text: Whoa! Hey, watch it! / There you go again. Shazbot!
jokes.setup: Movie trivia characters appearing
jokes.punchline: Mork's signature catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Simple nostalgia reference - not particularly clever but recognizable

jokes.text: If it spins forever, we're still in a dream.
jokes.setup: Testing if they're still dreaming
jokes.punchline: Inception reference about dream reality testing
jokes.explanation: Another direct Inception reference - relies too heavily on pop culture knowledge

jokes.text: Hey, since it's a dream, let's ride our bikes naked around town. Sounds like a plan.
jokes.setup: Family thinks they're still dreaming
jokes.punchline: Immediate plan for inappropriate behavior
jokes.explanation: Typical Bart mischief with Homer's enabling, setup for final gag

jokes.text: Is that hail coming down? It's just dream hail. Ow! Ooh! Ow! Dream hail!
jokes.setup: Family thinks painful hail is part of dream
jokes.punchline: Real consequences of thinking they're still dreaming
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy ending - family suffering real consequences while thinking it's still a dream

tmdb_overview: When Homer's bed-wetting worsens, his family tries to infiltrate his dreams to find the source of the problem.
